author: @LaylaSoleil
text: This analysis is not complete without fully understanding Morocco's point of view. Moroccan foreign policy is mainly driven by a 50 years long territorial dispute over Morocco's southern province ( Western Sahara ), fought against neighbouring Algeria and its proxy called  the Polisario front. Oil rich Algeria is backed by Russia and can afford to spend way more than Morocco when it comes to military spending. In 2020, Morocco has been granted Trump's recognition over Western Sahara and access to advanced military technologies in exchange of normalisation with Israel. Despite most Moroccans sympathising with the Palestinian cause; Algeria constantly threatening Moroccan security and territorial sovereignty is seen as a major national issue. Morocco officially backs a two state solution where both Israel and Palestine can prosper peacefully.

In the modern era actual military powers that exist outside of paper are extremely rare, the United States, Russia, Israel, Are among a handful of ful of countries that regularly see conflict and deploy weapons, actual combat experience is more essential than almost any other part of a military, other military’s are completely untested. Having an ally that actually knows how to deploy field and feed and military is so rare that it has become essentially the main concern of Europe at the moment because they have realized they have zero actual power outside of treaties, that’s why they are fighting so hard to support Ukraine because of Ukraine falls it shows that people can simply disobey the rules based order without consequences. author: @BanjoLuke1
text:
I'll never know, but I wonder sometimes whether Rains' character was so named because of Louis Renault and his company.
I'm not sure how widely Renault's passive (?) collaboration with the German occupiers was known in Hollywood at the time this film was written/made.
However... Captain Renault lives in Vichy Casablanca in the very same fog of deceit and pretence that Louis Renault used to profit from occupation. 
I may be wrong.
But I do wonder...

text: Nothing like that Silver Nitrate black and white film stock. It created all those wonderful shadows and made moonlight and water glow. It contained actual silver but of course it was very flammable. If you compare an old silver nitrate scene with a modern day black and white filmed scene, the modern one looks absolutely flat. No depth, no glow.

author: @jackrice2770
text:
I see a few film students have been assigned this film. They should note a couple of important things: 

1) Note there isn't any 'filler', not one unnecessary scene. 

2) Instead of the modern film style with naked, sweaty bodies flopping around for several minutes, here we see symbols and subtle shots that tell us that yes, Ilsa and Rick "do it" but we're spared the pace-killing soft-core porn so prevalent today. (That shot of the searchlight when the camera cuts away). Yes, Bogie and Ilsa are wearing clothes in the next shot, but apparently folks in the '40s were a bit quicker on the uptake than today. 

3) The script is tight, and although at least three writers worked on it, the final result works perfectly. The sign of a director in complete control of his project.

4) The shadows and camera angles are works of genius. Lighting designers and cinematographers should watch it frame by frame.

author: @jackrice2770
text:
Yes, in case you're a young'un, this movie has been voted one of the best by any number of film associations, One historical note: Renault says to Strasser that "he was with the Americans when they blundered into Berlin in 1918." Germany was never invaded during the First World War, and certainly no foreign troops, American or otherwise, entered the capital. Of course, one shouldn't rely on films for historical facts (actually, almost all 'historical' films are grossly inaccurate).

Here's looking at you, Kid.

author: @timothydavis1885
text: There have been few movies that have equaled the romantic twists, the suspense, the drama, the musical soundtrack, as Casablanca.   As Time Goes By will go down in cinematic history as one of the greatest musical masterpieces of all time.   This was undoubtedly Humphrey Bogart's greatest work. Humphrey Bogart was unquestionably one of the greatest actors ever to grace movie screen.  Ingrid Bergman will forever be one of the most beautiful woman ever to cross Hollywood, whose theatrical performance in Casablanca seemed as though she were living out her own life, which just happened to be portrayed in this timeless work of cinematic art.   Paul Heinreid was the ultimate patriot, a part he played so well, it would go down as one of the most realistic performances ever.  This movie is without doubt one of the top 10 movies Hollywood ever produced.

text: Rick wasn't helping Victor and Ilsa until she explained her train station no-show. He finally got that, then had to get Victor out of Captain Reynaud's custody and set up the pair's clean getaway. Rick realized that him and Ilsa having the hots for each other paled beside the importance of Victor's mission, and that staying together in Casablanca was impossibly dangerous. The script writers made sure Rick didn't have any easy outs, just a tough one.

text: "Kid" was colloquial of the time, like "kiddo" and "Bud". Rick wasn't calling her a child or a young goat. Bogart was 42, playing 37. Bergman was 27, playing 20-something, not 15. From what I've read, "Here's looking at you, kid" was what Bogart said when playing poker and calling for the cards to be shown. He used it here as a toast or pet phrase of endearment.

author: @rizqbeckett7086
text: @cameroncordova6448I have to disagree. The obvious thing is the age difference. Both men seem to be way older than her, when she explained to Rick how they found her and explained that Victor had escaped and was wounded, she sounded compromised because she was in fact a married woman, which clearly leaves the door open to speculate that her relationship with Rick was sexual. Now the nature of Victor’s injury was vague, but it was mentioned that he was tortured. So let’s just say for shits and giggles that the Nazis had tortured him that resulted in lifelong impotence, she wouldn’t have known that until she went back to him. And of course she had no way of knowing that when they arrived in Casablanca, and was informed that they were to meet someone who had the all important letters of transit at Rick’s Cafe Americana, that she would first see Sam and then Rick. I believe that prior to them running into each other she had accepted a sexless existence with a man that she loved. Until…

author: @tiffsaver
text:
@Rob-157 
For so many years I couldn't understand the attraction for Bogart, who was old, ugly, and short.  Not only that, but he had a dental problem that gave him a decidedly odd diction problem.  But after seeing him as 'Rick,' suddenly all of my petty criticisms disappeared.  I think Bogie was the single best example of "the tough guy with a heart of gold" that Hollywood ever produced.  Just watch his Academy Award performance in "The African Queen."  It was pure magic.

author: @tiffsaver
text: What actor today could have ever replaced these screen icons, from Humphry Bogart, to Ingrid Bergman, to Peter Lorre, to Sidney Greenstreet, to Claude Rains??  Absolutely NO ONE.

author: @anglophils645
text: This one should never be remade.  No one could come close to duplicating the performances of Bogart, Bergman, Henried, or Rains.  These actors each had such a unique style------I don't believe that any combination of actors today could come together the way that they did.  The re-make of "Sebrina" fell flat.  Even with stars like Harrison Ford, Richard Kinnear, and Julia Ormond, the 1995 version does not compare to the 1954 original, starring Humphrey Bogart, William Holden, and Audrey Hepburn.  I fear the same would happen with an attempt to re-make "Casablanca".

author: @anglophils645
text: @tiffsaverThe only classic that I can think of, where the re-make is possibly as good or better than the original, is Pride and Prejudice.  The original starring Greer Garson and Lawrence Olivier, is not necessarily better than the 1995 version starring Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle.  The 2005 version starring Keira Knightley and Donald Sutherland, among others, is also very good.

author: @tiffsaver
text:
@anglophils645 
Same thing with sequels.  But for my money, the best, and very first sequel of all time is "The Bride of Frankenstein," in 1935.

author: @J_Ges
text:
Hi Chris, compliments on your videos, I really enjoy them!
I’m a street photographer living in London and in March I’m going for a week in Marrakech. I’ve heard that sometimes people can be a bit hostile if they notice they’re being photographed, or may ask for money. I always try to be very respectful, quiet and discreet when I shoot, but I was wondering what your honest opinion is. If I’m travelling alone for a week, do you think a city like Rabat or Fez might be a better option for street photography, with people being a bit less confrontational? I’d really appreciate your thoughts. Thanks again for the great content.

author: @georgimihov2690
text: Old graves by/on the beach is actually a very common thing around the world - sunbathing and spending time on the beach is only a thing of the modern era. In previous centuries people had no use for beaches, as they are not arable land, so instead of creating cemeteries on good fertile land inland they would put them along a beach, or on the beach in some instances, to make use of otherwise useless space.

author: @DriveWithMina
text:
I totally understand the travel tiredness. Burnout happens to the best of us. Yes, you get to travel for a living, but at the same time, it's also work, and because it's work, it may drain your energy to actually enjoy what you wanted to do in the first place, travel. But thanks for being real with us! (And if you ever needed a break from posting videos, we'd understand! Mental health is important). Still, looking forward to your next adventure after this Morocco series. You def made Morocco high on my list of places to visit next. 

Rabat almost seems quiet and peaceful, and not so many people, which is always interesting for a capital city.

author: @Boug0014
text: It’s quite interesting with those street guides.  Some (I’m not saying all), don’t even know the history …. The Portuguese never occupied Rabat…. They occupied Maamoura in 1515 about 30km north of Rabat and Casablanca in also 1515.  But they never were in Rabat.  The Oudaya Kasbah was built by the Almohad Royal Family in 1150 as a fortification (Ribat) to fight the Berghwata Kingdom that extended from south of Rabat to about Essaouira.  The Kasbah was later used as a settlement for the Andalus Refugees after the fall of Granada and the expulsion of the Moriscos from Spain.

text: A beautiful, clean city; there isn't a single piece of litter in the streets. All the streets are spotless, and the luxurious buildings are all white. تقارن عاصمة الانوار المغربية الرباط التاريخية والنظيمة والمتطورة بمدينة تصنف باقدر واخطر المدن في العالم الا وهي الجزائر العاصمة هههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههههه المدينة المكان الوحيد المعروف فيها وهي المباني الفرنسية وماتبقى كله خراب وتلون واوساخ وعمران مخرب ولا اتر للعمران التاريخي وحتى القصبة مخربة جلها مباني مهدومة واخرى في طور الهدم وايلة للسقوط 
فعلا احس بك عندما تدخل بعد صراع عنيف مع ضميرك الدي يرفض دخولك ومشاهدة مدن المغرب وتنصدم بالفرق الشاسع بيننا وبينكم يا كرغولي وتخرج خائب منزعش في قلبك الحسد والغيرة ههههههههههههههههههههه

   لا تغطي الشمس الغربال                                        لم تحصل على شهادة السلامة العقلية منذ عام 1962. منذ القدم، لا تزال الحظيرة طوابير   النفط والغاز تعيش في الستين.                                 المغرب بلد بلا نفط ولا غاز. ويستورد المغرب النفط والغاز ومشتقاتهما بمليارات الدولارات. ومع ذلك، فإنه يزدهر ويوفر هذا. هذا هو الفرق بين المغرب الحظيرة البترول والغاز وطوابير  و شعارات فارغة.

author: @RightToLive3222
text:
Morroco is a corrupt country. The law and constitution is broken. 

Law and order only exists if bribes are paid. 

The court system is rigged where you always fall short of their demands. 

I was a landlord in Marrakesh. I tried to evict the tenant, the case is still active even after 4 years.

This was not a murder case. This was a case of evicting a tenant from my property. 

I would urge anyone not to invest in Morroco as the people and system is corrupt and broken.

author: @DLPdiaries
text: Marrakech is so amazing! We stayed in a Riad in the Medina and did excursions to the Atlas mountains (blew our mind, so beautiful!), Essouira and a quad tour through the palmeraie. And of course wandered through the medina, had the best food and experiences in the gardens and djamaa El Fna etc. I would realy reccomend visiting. The Moroccans are SO welcoming and kind! You do need to keep in mind to haggle in the shops, and not fall for the monkey scams on the square or ' i'll show you the way' scams but other than that...such amazing people and such a beautiful culture!

author: @ArjunLSen
text: Marrakesh is boring, the people working in the tourism industry are patronising and unfriendly, and you can get really ill eating the food. It was a massive disappointment and the most overrated destination I've been to in recent years. I live in Spain and there's more historic buildings in your average Spanish town than in the city of Marrakesh. The whole place is a big sell and a big con.  Avoid Marrakesh and probably Morocco. Africa is a big place and there are a lot more interesting and better value places to visit.

author: @truthsetyoufree104
text: The destinations you've explored don't capture the full essence of Morocco. A significant portion of the population grapples quietly with challenges like unemployment, inadequate education and healthcare, poverty, and mental health struggles. Additionally, a number of individuals, driven by desperation, risk their lives attempting to migrate to Europe. This is the stark reality that some influencers may overlook or choose not to share, but global awareness is growing, and the broader truths are increasingly hard to conceal.

author: @bravo2966
text:
@CelticMickTravels Sounds good. I'm torn for 2026 whether to use Amsterdam as a base, hire a car and visit a lot of WW2 sites over Northern Europe or do Marakech.
As the airport for Amsterdam is a 10 minute drive from my house and the Marakech airport would be London, a 5-6 hour drive for me, I'm leaning towards Amsterdam atm. From same local airport to Marakech it's like £400 more than the London price and has a layover...so a bit of a bugger whichever way I do it.

author: @worldwildhearts
text: When you arrive at Marrakech Airport, Moroccan immigration officers will ask for your passport and may inquire about the purpose of your visit and the duration of your stay. They might also ask for proof of onward travel, such as a return flight ticket, and sometimes the address of where you will be staying.

author: @ReigneOrShine
text: I said in the video that I was overwhelmed with the noise, crowd and fellow tourists. I have a love-hate relationship with the market. And you have no right to say that we are dishonest in our video. We go out as early as possible to beat the crowd,  and heat and it is always quieter in the morning. It is your fault for going out late then complain if it’s crowded. You should know the culture of the places that you visit. We also gave tips in the video, which you would know, IF you watched and listened to be careful of scammers. We knew about that by watching other travel videos and reading blogs before going. Again, it was your fault for not preparing yourself mentally of all the possibilities. Tourists are there to learn their culture, not to change it. If you did not like your experience, you have no right to accuse us of being dishonest when we clearly mentioned everything. Next time, before you talk, you listen to everything we say and have better understanding. If you do not like the crowd, go in the mountains!

author: @ReigneOrShine
text:
It's Chano here answering your comment, I am from Spain and I grew up in a Christian family, in case you may think I am Muslim because I love travelling to Morocco and learning about their history and culture. 
There is not historic evidence about a church being located where the Koutoubia Mosque was built. But our Cathedral of Sevilla was built on top of a previous mosque, and the old minaret was transformed into the beautiful Giralda bell tower, which is considered the architectural sister of the Koutoubia Minaret. 
The Spanish Christians built many churches on top of mosques after the Reconquista of Al-Andalus, and the Arabs did the same before as well, building mosques on top of Roman temples and Visigothic churches. And the Romans did the same on top of other religions temples. It was very common to repurpose materials or sites from previous religious buildings.
If you try to spread misinformation in our content we will have to block you. We respect different religions and ideas, so please do not spam our comment section with hatred. Thanks.

author: @ReigneOrShine
text:
Hi Nassa.
Thanks a million for watching our video and for appreciating our work! 🙏🏽 We're very happy to read your positive feedback and very kind words. Comments like yours keep us motivated to continue working hard and to share more travel documentaries around the world in the future. 🎥💪🏽😊

Regarding the herbal infusion, we cannot remember the price, but it wasn't expensive at all, they said it was done with mint, but not sure if something else was included. We still have some crystals at home, we use them with very little quantity and put boiling water when we have very bad hay fever for example, it helps to open the nose and it feels refreshing in the throat too. We haven't drunk it anymore though, as it was too strong, and just inhaling the boiling water it is more than enough for us. 
The name they had on their sign is: Herboriste Des Amis, and they had the following phone number on the sign too: 024382076 (Morocco's international prefix: +212). Google Maps: https://share.google/KlQ2z5FO6dCXCQQfl
We advise you contact your doctor if you have some health issues before taking traditional medicines too. Hope that helps!

We wish you all the very best to you too, have a wonderful trip to Marrakech! 🇲🇦❤️😊 Let us know if you have other questions in other videos, we are always happy to share information about travelling with our viewers. Best regards!

author: @ReigneOrShine
text: Thanks for watching. I think we have showed in the video that most local people were very friendly to us. Of course, like in all the big cities in the world, you need to have common sense. We have explained in the video about how to behave in the souks to avoid issues. Take the usual safety measures like the ones you would take visiting big cities like Rome, Paris or Madrid, just to mention some big cities we have visited in recent years, where you have to keep your belongings safe in crowded places. Do not believe scammers approaching you in the middle of the street. Do not stay alone late at night in areas where there are not many people. In our whole trip in Morocco we felt safe, you can watch the rest of our travel series to have a broader idea. We caught public buses (even at night), the train, we stayed after sunset and woke up for sunrise. Not a single issue. We met some wonderful people who just wanted to chat on the streets too. If someone suddenly offers you to bring you somewhere or to sell you something, in any country in the world, just don't trust them. If they are just curious and friendly, be open to a nice conversation. 🙂

text: Thanks for watching! We walked everywhere in Marrakech, but we visited the Jardin Majorelle on our last day in the city at the end of the whole Morocco trip. author: @ReigneOrShine
text:
Thank you for watching our video!

We didn´t exchange money in Morocco, we have a Revolut card that allow us to withdraw money for free in other countries up to 200€ per month, and then after that we pay only 1€ commission for every withdrawal. But you have to find the proper ATMs or Banks in every country to avoid paying commissions there. In Morocco, we only used the Al Barid Bank ATMs and we never paid commission with them. I would advise you to find them in Google Maps in every city in advance to save their location, there are not that many and better be prepared with enough cash, but if you are only visiting Marrakech, you have an Al Barid Bank ATM at the airport in the departures area (walk to the right side immediately before exiting out the airport). Then you have 2 or 3 more Al Barid Bank ATMs in the Jemma el-Fnaa square just outside the Post Office building. Sometimes we could pay in Restaurants and Hotels directly with our Revolut debit card. They always have great currency exchange.

We often show dishes with meat as you can see in our video, but we remember that in almost every restaurant that we show in this video, they had vegetarian dishes as well, you can find easily falafel, vegetarian couscous or vegetarian tajine. The tacos can be found without meat as well and they often have soups and salads done with only vegetables. So I think Morocco is a good country for a vegetarian to try local cuisine as well. And don´t forget their desserts! ☺️

Hope our reply was useful. Wish you a wonderful trip to Marrakech! 🇲🇦❤️

author: @ReigneOrShine
text:
Hi Kitiwan, 
Thank you very much for watching our Morocco Travel Series and for appreciating our work. We´re really glad to know that the videos have been useful for preparing your coming trip to Morocco. You will love it there for sure! 🇲🇦❤️😊
So, ladies can wear whatever they want in public, in fact, sometimes we saw some local young women showing their legs with modern outfits, and many tourists were wearing short dresses as well. But at the same time, Morocco is a muslim country and they have certain cultural traditions and I preferred to respect that during our visit. I had heard some women receiving bad comments for dressing too sexy while visiting Morocco, but I only received smiles & nice comments about my dresses and the locals were often happy to see me with certain clothes. My husband helped me to choose some dresses before we visited, because he had already been there and he also knew the best colours for matching with the architecture 😅
In December, it may be cold early in the morning and at night too, so be careful with that, specially in the north of the country and in the desert. The Moroccan sun is very strong during the day, so traditional long clothing is actually the best for the desert for example. 
Hope my answer helped you a little bit!
All the best and wish you have a wonderful trip there!

author: @ReigneOrShine
text: Hello there Tom! There are indeed scams but for us they are very obvious. Also it was a great help that we watched a lot of videos before going there so we already knew what to avoid. For example, a random man who says that the way is closed then he will guide us to the correct way. Then he will ask money when you will arrive to the place. Do not believe them and just say no thank you. A lot of the merchants will be very friendly to you too and ask where are you from to start a conversation. We just answered them politely but we kept walking to where we were headed. If you know are lost for example in the souks because it’s like a labyrinth, just ask a merchant and probably avoid asking a random guy in the street. We were fine. We avoided “rough” areas. We used common sense. They are not going to hurt you. Some of them are just trying to earn money. Just ignore or say thank you. We felt safe in general. A lot of them are very friendly too. We met a family who just wanted to speak and practice their English. We are still in contact with them and invited us to visit their town.

author: @ReigneOrShine
text:
About the second question, Sardinia and Morocco are totally different. It depends on what you want to experience.

Sardinia is for beautiful Mediterranean coasts, coastal towns, chill life, amazing well kept nature, friendly locals, good food.

Morocco if you want to experience North African culture, more lively chaotic but unique culture, good food, mix of African and European architecture. And by the way, Moroccan architecture is impeccable and incomparable. They have one of the best designs I’ve ever seen. As a matter of fact, I want a touch of Moroccan design in my future house. Haha!! 

So if you decide which ones you want to experience, choose one. If you have more questions, feel free to ask us. We have Sardinia videos too.

author: @ReigneOrShine
text: Also, be careful of other videos describing the scams in Morocco. We found them too exaggerated. We’re not saying that scamming doesn’t exist but sometimes they were too much. And I think it is becoming more and more famous through social media so they are more careful now too.

author: @paullefko
text:
Rent a car; hire a driver; go see the real Morroco and avoid the tourist stuff. A car is about 400 a week a driver is 150 a day. Not only are you wasting time sitting on train, you’re missing out on cities and towns along the journey. And by taking “tours” you’re locked into an agenda and if you want to explore more, well you can’t. 
Cash is king there; credit cards are rarely used around the country. 

Honnêtement, la vidéo est sympa, mais elle ne rend pas justice à l'authenticité du Maroc. Si vous y retournez, sortez de votre zone de confort touristique. J'apprécie que vous ayez opté pour des visites guidées par des locaux ; trouvez maintenant un guide local qui vous emmènera découvrir des endroits hors des sentiers battus.

Real tips: if you see it a line of tourists to go in… yeah pass; 2. Restaurants- the more the ranking and tourists… yeah no ;go to mom and pops. 
Coffee is awesome; but do an expresso with an Americano with milk. Big boost in taste. 
Tea is meant to sipped slowly
Don’t eat with your left hand, Moroccans don’t necessarily use utensils.

Demandez aux gens de  prendre en photo; beaucoup refusent. Utiliser de gros appareils photo et du matériel vidéo est très mal vu ; vous avez probablement attiré beaucoup de regards à cause de votre équipement.

Sahara desert, oasis, woods, snow, rivers, two water fronts long ocean coastline and mediterranean golden sand. surf spot, mountains, old medinas, vibrant souks, festivals, colorful cities etc.. He has too a rich culture of food, music, hand craft, traditionnel clothing, home furnitur, interior design, tiles etc.. As you can see in Marrakech city as berber saharien or in Tanger as mauresque with some spanish archetecture touch, the city that contains the world's first United States embassy in the history. Also kingdom of Morocco is the first country to recognize USA in the world at 1777. We are so open as civilisation for exemple about the spanish influences, they were in the north of Morocco for almost 50 yeras but not problem becaus we were in their country for 800 years 😊 that's pretty much isn't ?! lool. We left the last city Grenada at january of 1492, Just a few month later, they discovered America at october of 92 by Columbus using our tools. they translated too our books left in the university of Cordoba the best university in the world at that time. Two years after Spain become the new bigest super power on earth with Portugal. Why not ?! nothing was missing 🙂. Just for information it's the empire of Morocco who built almost 60 % of spanish cities. for exemple Garnata city become Grenada, Ichbillia city become Sevilla, kortoba become Cordoba, Balancia become Valencia etc.. All this legacy of sciences has crossed borders of the Andalousie reached France then to England and Florence city in Italy by Sicily island to declare the edge of renaissance in europe. author: @artdirector9517
text: this country is safe you have just to : 1 - put your back-bag on your chest in the crowded souks. 2 - bargain is part of the culture so you can bargaining about every price, it's normal in souks, propos always the half price pretends to leave and wait for his reaction. 3 - if you get lost just ask kiosk owners or old age locals or their ki dss is better 😉 4 - it's so easy to rent a car, renting a car doesn't cost too much like 30 dollars. 5 - taxis is very sheap too from 1 dollar just ask the taxi driver to turn on the distance metter it's obligatory for him. 6 - if you look for an tasty first Tagine experience. the real Tagine is : is when cooked directly in Tagine pot in live, is when meat is sticky on honeyed form but better if it was cooked on coal. Some streets activities prices : Henee hand tatoe cost like 8 dollars. Tips for musicians like 2 dollars but if you film with them a dance, more. Tips for Sneaks charmer like 2 dollars but if you shoot a vlog video with them, more. Short Camel ride or pigmy horse ride from 5 dollars. the golden advice here is : fixed the price with each one of them before to make easier your travel. Just free you mind and joy it as you can. A great kingdom, beautiful country and a welcoming people good luck 👍 my purpose is avoiding problems and misunderstandings between tourists and locals because I feel sad when I see a vlog that deals with small incidents that don't even deserve to be anxious about them, especially when traveling. Tips (few money) is nothing compared to a moment of joy. life is too short to live it with anxiety. Peace and love 🙏 nice vlog 👍 keep going. '.'.'.'.''.''.'.'

author: @NajahAzzouzi
text: What I also found insulting is your partner’s comment on the pastries you got from Haj Belkabir. Saying “I wonder how old these are??!” Is just unacceptable and implies that being sold old pastries and getting conned is expected in Marrakech! These mistakes completely undermined your video, what could otherwise have been a great documentary due to its graphic quality.

author: @NajahAzzouzi
text: While I appreciate the attention you gave Morocco and Marrakech, I was quite annoyed by the way you handled and reported the snake charmers’ situation. Holding 20 dirhams (the equivalent of $2) in your hands, like some sort of protective strategy to get the snake charmers to let you film them alone was insulting. Then, you went on to describe the interaction like some sort of primitive encounter (that was not expected how it unfolded, you said). They took your camera to film the cobra up close, and you ended up with a great shot!! That didn’t seem to redeem them in your eyes. As to putting a snake around your neck, that’s standard practice, and, I believe, you must have researched it prior to going. I don’t think you said that out of malice, yet your “ classic Western tourist approach” is insulting and reinforces the civilizational/colonial prejudices.

author: @jgvarea
text:
Greetings. 
The sweets you show at 9:15 are mass-produced and don't represent the exquisite Moroccan pastry.
They contain more peanuts than almonds and sugary syrups instead of honey, and the flours are usually of poor quality.
To enjoy true Moroccan pastries, you have to go to Pastiseria Le Gato in Marrakech, among many others; they're nothing like what you find in the souks.

author: @ThomasRebelo-s3e4n
text:
Marrakesh is like the NEw York of Morocco .  The Medina is full of tourists milling around and the Medina is a constant hum of mopeds and scooters buzzing up the middle.  She said occasional i would say constant.  

  anywhere but Marrakesh would be my suggestion.  Fez or Chefchouan for an authentic experience.   Found a hamman in Marrakesh near the Grand old Mosque.  The old islamic college worth a visit.  The Bahia Palace under construction so sights limited.  I agree with photography paradise.  stay away from snake charmers..big scam.
but remember:  ripping off tourist , or worst hurting or assaulting a tourist is jail time, no questions ask.   So no matter what they demamd from u they wont do anything to you.  Military dictatorships are great for tourists. 

Negotiating is their national sport.  You will get ripped off (relatively speaking) they are just better that you.  you will end up buying a carpet u didnt think you needed.  the best way is to sample a price for let us say a leather bag. go to 3 different shops to get a sample price then you will have an idea what the price would be .  offer half and be ready to walk away .  u gotta be kind of rude . 
as for food , go outside Medina and you will find a grill smell.  look for a big pot on counter , it means soup is available for 10 MAD.  
it is a great country ,  people are generally nice ....explore the towns outside marrakesh

author: @KristinasTravels
text:
Apologies, this was not our intention. 
The sweets joke was just comic relief, as we don’t have open air sweet stores in Australia, so it’s just something that came to mind and we had a little chuckle about. Of course we didn’t think they were actually old, otherwise we wouldn’t have eaten them. 

In regards to the train station, we actually had a couple of uncomfortable encounters onboard the train. One man tried to befriend us and asked many personal questions, only to be escorted out of our carriage as he didn’t have a valid ticket to be in our booth. We also had another 3 people be rowdy, eat greasy food and drink while on the lookout for the guard, only to be also kicked out of our carriage which also made us feel uncomfortable. So, these situations, while they can happen everywhere, and in any country, did happen to us first hand. We chose not to include these stories in our report. 

Thank you for watching all the same, we had a fabulous time in Morocco, but we were also very much out of our comfort zone at times, but this is just one experience, and our experience. I’m sure everyone has a different story to tell 🙂

text: I see this was made in 2010. Many big changes in Tangier road systems since then and there is a project to 'join up' Tangier and Malabata bays. The view will be amazing as cruise liners come into port. If anyone is up, that is. They tend to come in before dawn. The medina is just the same. Oh, and the clubs and bars along the corniche have all been demolished for a vision of a 'clean' Tangier which is family friendly. That area will also look on to a marina. The fishing port is being shunted sideways. They're supposed to be trying to keep the walls of the Kasbah but they look very precarious.

text:
Oh yeah here in Denmark where im from, most people can not imagine going to the US on holidays anymore. Us Danes in general already hate Trump more than almost anyone else on earth for his corruption, sexism, opposition to science and international law, and whole centre of the world attitude thinking he can undermine anyone else. We've of course felt the brunt of that with his lash outs over wanting to invade Greenland. 

But even outside of that, many also fear for their safety regarding the US. Especially about ICE, given stories if people being withheld in ICE camps for over a month just for minor technicalities, or people being turned around at the border for having ever dared to criticize their "gloriest greatest leader Donald J Trump". Its downright north korean conditions. Those few that do travel to the US often rent out burner phones with absolutely no personal info on them just to be sure they can even get in. Its insane, simply put!

Its not really that Danes have anything against the average American (though many are turning a side eye to anyone who'd even consider voting for Mr Grab em by the puss puss) but they just cannot support the US in what theyre doing now.

author: @balthazarbeutelwolf9097
text: There is only a very small group of foreign tourists who are so political that the dislike of a particular government would deter them from travel. However, when there is a sense that those government's policies could directly impact their journey that changes drastically. Perhaps there is a non-neglegible risk that you are rejected at the border, forfeiting your hotel booking, never mind the transatlantic flight in vain. The erraticness of some of Trump's policies is also an issue: perhaps your visa-free status is revoked all of a sudden and you are supposed to fork out for an expenditure that was not on your bingo card. Producing your 5-year-history of social media was an idea floating in the air, seriously? What would that even entail for the permanently off-line? Besides politics, there were also stories of part of the US tourism industry fleecing world cup travellers, e.g. New Jersey train ticket prices going up 5-fold.

author: @StoryWeavers0102
text:
私は日本という国に深い敬意と関心を持っています。
日本の規律ある社会、時間を大切にする姿勢、そして努力する人が正当に評価される文化に強く惹かれています。
私は現在、バングラデシュに住んでおり、17歳です。
高校10年生として勉強している学生で、学業には真剣に取り組んでいます。
自分の努力と責任感によって、将来をより良いものにしたいと考えています。
残念ながら、私の国では腐敗や詐欺、賄賂といった社会的な問題が多く、
誠実に努力する人が十分に評価されにくい現実があります。
そのため、私は努力・誠実さ・規律が尊重される環境で
自分自身を成長させたいと強く思うようになりました。
私は日本で日本語と文化を学び、
将来的には社会の一員として責任を果たし、
法律を守り、周囲に良い影響を与えられる人間になりたいと考えています。
この目標を達成するために、日本語学習は不可欠です。
もしご指導やサポートをいただける機会があれば、
常に感謝の気持ちを忘れず、誠実に努力することをお約束します。

author: @Nadeshiko-Lover
text:
The *ORDER* in Japan all stems from the Tokugawa Shogunate which locked down Japan for TWO-HUNDRED-SIXTY-FIVE years (1603-1868) meaning that no one entered or exited the country whatsoever, during which period they all worked together and all taught themselves everything from fishing to farming to martial arts and everything in between. 


The *CIVILITY* in Japan all stems from Gen. Douglas MacArthur who, after Japan surrendered and ended WWII, moved into the HOTEL NEW GRAND in Yokohama and who was not an evil tyrant, but who gave Japan its own Constitution and whose men were all picked for their own manners and charm, not to mention for their good looks.^


The *QUALITY* in Japan all stems from an American Statistician named *DR. W. EDWARDS DEMING* who was sent there after WWII by the US Government under a two year contract, to conduct a Census, but after work most evenings he would go to various nightclubs and play the “guitar, piano and drums and also sing and dance!” In the process he made many friends and upon completion of his contract the Government of Japan asked him to stay behind and teach them about quality to which he amassed the top twenty-one Industrialists in the country and explained both how to *MEASURE* quality and how to *CONTROL* quality to which the Government of Japan - through the J.U.S.E. - established the “DEMING PRIZE COMMITTEE” medal so to this day, the Highest Award that anyone in Japan can win for quality is named after an American! 


^As an aside, when he arrived in Japan MacArthur sequestered himself in said “Hotel New Grand” at at his first dinner there with his staff he said, *”Boys, this is the greatest adventure in military history. author: @LahlouAbde-f7j3n
text: The Senegalese national team displayed a blatant lack of respect towards the FIFA president, the CAF president, the tournament organizing committee, and the senior sports officials present at the stadium during the final match between Senegal and Morocco, the host country of the 2025 CAF World Cup in the Kingdom of Morocco. The players left the pitch for the locker room for approximately fifteen minutes. They may have showered, had a drink, or used their phones before returning to finish the match with utter insolence and disrespect. Their entry and exit from the pitch mid-match, without any legal basis, freely and without sanction, constitute the greatest disgrace in the history of world football. Furthermore, they provoked unrest and clashes between Senegalese supporters and Moroccan security forces, tarnishing what was arguably the best and most impressive tournament ever held in Africa. However, these actions were orchestrated and instigated by parties hostile to Morocco in order to sabotage Moroccan and continental celebrations. Therefore, severe sanctions are necessary, and the law must be enforced. This should serve as a lesson for the future of African and world football.

author: @sandirlo
text:
They walked off the pitch for 15 damn minutes, crying like victims, while two Moroccan security agents were killed and journalists had their equipment destroyed. Yet somehow, when Moroccan players are forced to deal with chaos and provocation, they’re painted as the villains.

This narrative is irrational and disgusting. From the first whistle, the world rushed to judge Morocco instead of looking at the facts. Senegal has already pulled similar scenes against Egypt and Tunisia — and now they do it again in an AFCON final, on the biggest stage, knowing exactly how the media will spin it.

Morocco didn’t create the disorder — they endured it. But once again, Moroccans are expected to stay silent while others play victim and rewrite reality. Enough. Truth matters, and Morocco deserves fairness, not scapegoating.

author: @Andalus4Ever
text:
Let’s look at it from a regulatory standpoint, because several key concepts are being misunderstood.

First: the argument that “the referee’s decision is final” is being misapplied.

Yes, under Law 5, the referee’s decisions during the match are final in terms of on-field play.

But that does not mean the result is immune from post-match disciplinary review.

That’s a completely separate layer of governance.

Now, the core issue here is straightforward: a team leaving the field of play without the referee’s permission constitutes a regulatory breach.

And the rules on that are not ambiguous:

- Leaving the field without authorization is considered a serious violation

- It can lead to a forfeit, regardless of what happens afterward

So the argument that “they didn’t leave the stadium, only the pitch” is irrelevant.

The obligation is to participate in the match on the field of play, not merely to remain somewhere inside the venue.

Now, here’s the key misunderstanding:

“The game restarted, so everything before that is erased.”

That is simply incorrect.

Football governance clearly distinguishes between:

- The sporting outcome on the field, and

- The regulatory outcome after review

A referee allowing play to continue does not nullify a prior breach of the rules.

Disciplinary bodies exist precisely to address situations where infractions occurred, regardless of how the match was managed in real time.

Let me put it in a simple analogy:

Imagine a marathon runner who takes a shortcut off the official route, then re-enters the race and crosses the finish line first. At the moment, officials might not stop them. The race “finishes.”

But once the violation is reviewed, the result is corrected — because the rules of the competition were broken.

The same principle applies here.

Finally, claims of “disgrace,” “illegality,” or “backroom decisions” are speculative and not substantiated by the regulatory framework. These decisions are made within established procedures, and they remain subject to appeal — including at CAS.

So the bottom line is this:

Emotions about fairness are understandable.
But they do not override the rules.

And the rules are clear:
if a team leaves the field of play without authorization, it exposes itself to disciplinary sanctions — including forfeiture — even if the match was later resumed.

That’s not opinion.
That’s the regulatory framework.

Dima Maghrib ❤️🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦❤

author: @AntoinetteSarpong
text: Congrats! How often were you posting each day?

author: @Ohnoplata12
text:
​@AntoinetteSarpongmy rule for myself is don't go more than 2 days without a reel/post. Could I potentially grow more if I was posting multiple times a day, making multiple stories, going live, commenting on similar posts to mine as soon as I make a new post, and everything else all the gurus say? Yes probably. 

But, the reality Is that when I was doing that? I enjoyed it for all of the week or two and then it just turned me off or I'd become uninterested. Now I do three to five posts a week, I'm not worrying about thumbnails and cool editing. I'm just focusing on consistency. And I'm happy how it's slowly and authentically growing.

author: @AntoinetteSarpong
text: @Ohnoplata12 yeah, that makes sense! I’m a small creator as well and I’m finding the same cadence is what I can do consistently.

author: @sofianechenaoui4046
text:
Salut ! Je suis avec beaucoup de plaisir tes aventures à Marrakech et Agadir. En tant que Libanais vivant en France, c'est super de voir ton regard sur notre beau pays.
​Si tu en as l'occasion, il faut absolument que tu viennes faire un tour à Casablanca ! C'est le cœur battant du Maroc. Je serais ravi de t'accueillir et de te faire visiter la région pour te montrer une autre facette de l'ambiance marocaine : son énergie débordante, son mélange unique de modernité et de traditions, et surtout, la légendaire hospitalité casablancaise.
​On t'attend pour te faire découvrir la vraie vie de Casa et ses pépites cachées. À très bientôt j'espère !"

text: Pour tous futur vacancier sachez que le Maroc n est pas le pays paradisiaque il renferme comme ds tous les pays du monde des virus et des microbes mais la majorité ont une morale ok de plus c est pas parce que le pays est en voie de développement que vous devez vous attendre à des prix extrêmes bas tous vacances à un prix aussi quand on visite un pays on le dénigre pas en prenant les virus et microbes pour en faire une généralité faire une vidéo choquant pour pouvoir rentabiliser ses vacances quand on a pas d argent on reste chez soi a bon entendeur le Maroc c est un pays touristique il faut prévoir le budget à croire que vous êtes mieux lotis dans votre propre pays je me répète les arnaques c est ds tous les pays merci de bien vouloir respecter cette population bienveillante et d arrêtez de mettre en avant les virus et microbes pour rentabiliser votre voyage eh ds la vie rien est gratuit j ai le sentiment que vous êtes tellement dans un profond mal être ds votre propre pays que vous attendez à ce que le peuple marocain vous apporte toutes la reconnaissance que vous recherchez les marocains ne sont pas responsable de votre mal être  de plus ce ne sont pas vos esclaves pour avoir le tapis rouge il faut le respect et le budget le Maroc n est ni gratuit  ni le marocain va se priver pour votre propre bien être ils sont très hospitalier mais pas les prendre pour des esclaves  c est un pays touristique il faut poser l argent de plus les policiers sont à chaque coin de rue vous rencontrez un problème ils sont là pour votre protection et vos droits personne ne peut abuser de vous la police est là pour ça ils font leur job ok les personnes qui ont le goût de voyager sont généralement ouvert d esprit de ce faites ils sont conscient de la misère mondiale et qu elle diffère d un pays à un autre de ce faite passé leurs temps a développé leur humanité et en aucun cas mettent en avant les défauts d un pays au contraire les points positifs pour inciter les autres à Il a clairement voulu vous arnaquer car il a l’habitude de prendre des clients dans le même secteur et le faire déplacer jusqu’en ville il voulait que vous lui repayer une course retour . C’est clairement du grand n’importe quoi ! Prenez Indrive la prochaine fois! Voilà je continue mon visionnage , bisous mes gadiris 😘🇲🇦

author: @tombushing4907
text:
@yololopata8925 Moi aussi, j’ai été très clair: le Sahara Occidental n’appartient pas au Maroc. Ce territoire est colonisé par le Maroc, par la force, contre le droit international. La résolution 2797 des Nations Unies que tu as citée ne nie pas le droit à l’autodétermination sous la supervision de l’ONU. Cette résolution ne dit pas que le Sahara Occidental est Marocain. Au contraire, elle reconnaît le droit du peuple Sahrawi de décider lui-même s’il souhaite rejoindre le Maroc ou non. Bien sûr, tous les citoyens Marocains qui ont envahi le Sahara Occidental juste après le départ des Espagnols ne sont pas Sahraouis. Par conséquent, leurs voix ne comptent pas.

Pour l’Algérie, elle n’a rien à y gagner. Elle ne revendique rien sur le Sahara Occidental. L’Algérie considère simplement cela comme une question coloniale. Ainsi, en tant que victime du colonialisme elle-meme, l’Algérie a des principes: aider les peuples à se libérer de l’occupation. Cela est inscrit dans la Charte des Nations Unies.

Le Polisario est un mouvement qui représente le peuple Sahraoui. Il est reconnu comme tel par ONU. Il n’est pas Algérien, contrairement à la délégitimation Marocaine de ce mouvement populaire. L’Algérie soutient le peuple Sahraoui par l’intermédiaire de son représentant légal, le Polisario, qui est reconnu dans les documents de l’ONU. Aucun des documents ou résolutions internationaux n’indique que le Polisario est Algérien. Seuls les Marocains, programmees par leur gouvernement et leurs médias, repetent ce qu'ils entendent, sans reflechir ou analyser les faits et la loi. 

Le simple fait que Trump et Israël aient échangé la reconnaissance d’Israël par le Maroc contre l’abandon du Sahara Occidental au Maroc ne rend pas cet accord légal. Ce territoire n’appartient ni à Israël ni aux États-Unis pour qu’ils le donnent au Maroc. Israël et les États-Unis sont tous deux des puissances coloniales vivant sur des terres volées, et le Maroc est aussi une puissance coloniale vivant sur des terres volées. Ainsi, une transaction entre voleurs est illégale en vertu de toute loi. Les États-Unis et Israël ont poussé à l’adoption de la résolution 2797, mais la Cour internationale de Justice a réaffirmé qu’une telle résolution ne nie pas le droit du peuple Sahraoui à l’autodétermination. Cela signifie que seul le peuple de ce territoire peut décider de son propre sort, pas le Maroc. Le Maroc n'as pas le droit to voler la terre des autres, c'est Haram en Islam.

text: @techinfosmr8179 Du “carnet sanitaire” délivré par l’administration coloniale aux f€mm€s enfermées dans le quartier réservé de B0usbir, jusqu’à la monnaie israéli€nne qui circule aujourd’hui dans certains quartiers de Marrakech, le mécanisme de fond reste identique : l’économie politique du corps féminin y est devenue une variable d’ajustement des alliances étrangères. D’une époque à l’autre, une seule constante traverse l’histoire : l’argent venu d’ailleurs dicte les orientations stratégiques du royaume, et les f€mm€s du pays en supportent, directement ou indirectement, le coût social et symbolique. Des légions r0maines aux dr0nes israéli€ns, le Maroc n’a jamais su opposer de refus souverain à ceux qui le modelaient selon leurs intérêts. Continuez donc à pavoiser derrière votre m0narchie. Mais sous l’orgueil de façade, cette longue tradition d’accueil forcé des occupants d’hier aux si0nist€s d’aujourd’hui ne révèle qu’une chose : la profonde vulnérabilité d’un peuple que l’on a trop longtemps habitué à se plier, faute d’avoir appris à se tenir debout par lui-même.

author: @user-oc8jp2bk2y
text:
I am not into this music genre, but why does this art feels like the essence of my childhood?

Huh, that was pretty intense
I barely listened to music, I just paused, started at the art, felt something and wrote that comment, and now I am editing it telling that apprently it helped me
This does convey the vibe of my childhood, and I had to reprocess it for a bit
My childhood mostly was not happy, and mostly, traumatic shit happened to happen at summers, so I spent most either gaming or walking with friends outside (most of them were not doing okay too)
So... I recalled some memories and had to cry for the first time about it, I would probably get to that later otherwise but it happened here

author: @drdewott9154
text:
Oh yeah here in Denmark where im from, most people can not imagine going to the US on holidays anymore. Us Danes in general already hate Trump more than almost anyone else on earth for his corruption, sexism, opposition to science and international law, and whole centre of the world attitude thinking he can undermine anyone else. We've of course felt the brunt of that with his lash outs over wanting to invade Greenland. 

But even outside of that, many also fear for their safety regarding the US. Especially about ICE, given stories if people being withheld in ICE camps for over a month just for minor technicalities, or people being turned around at the border for having ever dared to criticize their "gloriest greatest leader Donald J Trump". Its downright north korean conditions. Those few that do travel to the US often rent out burner phones with absolutely no personal info on them just to be sure they can even get in. Its insane, simply put!

Its not really that Danes have anything against the average American (though many are turning a side eye to anyone who'd even consider voting for Mr Grab em by the puss puss) but they just cannot support the US in what theyre doing now.

author: @balthazarbeutelwolf9097
text: There is only a very small group of foreign tourists who are so political that the dislike of a particular government would deter them from travel. However, when there is a sense that those government's policies could directly impact their journey that changes drastically. Perhaps there is a non-neglegible risk that you are rejected at the border, forfeiting your hotel booking, never mind the transatlantic flight in vain. The erraticness of some of Trump's policies is also an issue: perhaps your visa-free status is revoked all of a sudden and you are supposed to fork out for an expenditure that was not on your bingo card. Producing your 5-year-history of social media was an idea floating in the air, seriously? What would that even entail for the permanently off-line? Besides politics, there were also stories of part of the US tourism industry fleecing world cup travellers, e.g. New Jersey train ticket prices going up 5-fold.

author: @benanderson89
text:
I got grilled by immigration for hours. I flew into Toronto to stay with a friend, then we both got in his car to drive over the border to visit another. All paperwork was good, all fees paid... They stuck my passport in a numatic tube and barked at me to go into an opaque glass building where I sat with no outside contact, no food or water and fucking Fox playing on the television hung on the wall.

If we all want to see each other in a big group, the Europeans and Americans are going to Canada to stay with friends in Toronto, or the Canadians and Americans are flying over to Europe to stay with me in England or another friend in Germany.

I got *mild* treatment compared to some and it was still abject misery. Fuck the USA. Rest in Piss.

author: @hypergraphic
text:
I lived in South Africa for 8 years as an expat, and for the most part, I didn't have any problems with crime. Until one day, my family and a friend were coming out of a mall, and my friend accidentally got into the wrong car. The dude followed her back to our car,  who was armed and presented himself as a police officer who wanted to get us to follow him to the station for questioning. It was a tense hour or so of going back and forth, with the people from the mall doing nothing, until eventually we got a South African friend on the phone who told us he was a fake police officer and was trying to kidnap us!  You can limit yourself to only the safe areas and still find yourself in danger.

 I enjoyed my time in South Africa and made lots of good friends, but I'm glad to be back in the US, even though we have crime problems too. It's just a lot easier to defend yourself with deadly force, and by and large, you can rely on the police in many jurisdictions. South Africa really could be paradise if the right people were in charge.

author: @afrikalovers6640
text:
As a German immigrant who lives in and loves South Africa for many years, I have to acknowledge that your documentary serves as a shocking display of arrogant eurocentrism. 

Starting with a fairly decent review of South Africa’s most recent history, your journalism then deteriorates over an astonishing number of generic mistakes that any good journalist would have learned to avoid in his first semester. 

You seem to not have considered one African person or person of colour (who together make up over 90% of our population) in your “research”, you only speak to Afrikaaners as your source of first hand information and you really manage to not go to one place that is significant to more South Africans than the Afrikaaner minority (apart from the Apartheid Museum which you also manage to debaucher and misrepresent in its entirety). At this point it’s now obvious that you really didn’t want to make a documentary but a mockery of South Africa. 

Nothing takes you out of your intellectual bubble not even food, because you eat at establishments that imitate British and American style food. Only chain restaurants nothing remotely interesting. You attend at least one braai but you don’t even share the story of the family that invited you. Which shows once again that your journalism is not here to showcase its subject but rather to follow the narrator’s political agenda…

You skip a myriad of opportunities to engage with and experience the diversity of this beautiful country and instead go down the rabbit hole or Orania.

You focus on all the negatives which are obviously manifold in a country that is run by corrupt politicians like (guess what) any other country including yours and mine. And unfortunately the political elites are put in place by global financial elites, so most South Africans do not feel represented by them whatsoever. 

What is most sad in all of this is that many people will probably watch your more than close minded and one sided journalistic debauchery of this beautiful country (filled with so many wonderful people), and will believe you because you are very good at making your journalistic approach seem like it has any value other than promoting ignorance, separation and intolerance.

author: @lzot
text:
Not like the brazen giant of Greek fame,
With conquering limbs astride from land to land;
Here at our sea-washed, sunset gates shall stand
A mighty woman with a torch, whose flame
Is the imprisoned lightning, and her name
Mother of Exiles. From her beacon-hand
Glows world-wide welcome; her mild eyes command
The air-bridged harbor that twin cities frame.
"Keep, ancient lands, your storied pomp!" cries she
With silent lips. "Give me your tired, your poor,
Your huddled masses yearning to breathe free,
The wretched refuse of your teeming shore.
Send these, the homeless, tempest-tost to me,
I lift my lamp beside the golden door!"


Not any more.

author: @Asmarman3
text:
L'objectif n'est pas de se qualifier pour la Coupe du monde, mais de concourir pour le titre et de battre les grandes équipes. Les équipes arabes et africains sont loin de cela, à l'exception du Maroc, toujours premier dans tous les disciplines notamment de l' athlétisme du Aouita, et motawakil en 84 passant par le hicham el garouje salah hissou, bakali...la liste est longue.
En foot, le premier point pour les Arabes et l' Afrique dès leur première participation en 1970. En 1986, ils passent le premier tour à se qualifier en tant que leader du  groupe relevé. Enfin, ils sont les premiers à se qualifier pour les demi-finales de la Coupe du monde au Qatar en 2022. 
Aucune équipe arabe et africaine qui ne leur ressemble aujourd'hui, surtout depuis 2018. Le Maroc est le visage du football africain et arabe, non seulement parmi les grandes équipes, mais dans toutes les catégories : 
champion d'Afrique U17 à deux reprises et demi-finaliste de la dernière Coupe du monde en 2023. 
Champion d'Afrique U20, est désormais champion du monde en 2025 au Chili.  
champion d'Afrique U23, et médaillés de bronze olympiques en 2024 à Paris, 
l'équipe locale, triple championne d'Afrique d' affilié (2018, 2020 et 2024). 
Et enfin, l'équipe senior, quatrième au monde en demi-finale en 2022.
 Sans oublier les équipes féminines et de futsal, où le Maroc domine l'Afrique et le monde arabe avec sept coupes consécutives… Le Maroc est le seul pays à avoir d'avantages des talons dans tous les championnats continentaux et mondiaux…

author: @Will_4_Jesus
text:
Romans 7 when Paul says.

“For that which I do I allow not: for what I would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.”
— Romans 7:15 (KJV)

And later he says. 

“For I know that in me (that is, in my flesh,) dwelleth no good thing: for to will is present with me; but how to perform that which is good I find not.”
— Romans 7:18 (KJV

Paul knows we live in a body that is wrestling with our sin and Jesus took it and the sin is dead.

“Now then it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.”
— Romans 7:17 (KJV)

author: @Irish__Clan
text:
It reminds me of the question:  “If everything you believe in is a lie, would you want to know it?”

Many people answer “No” or “I don’t know”.  

The fear of one’s world view shattering is real and intense.  Theo asks great questions that actually lead somewhere.  Cognitive dissonance — when one becomes cognizant of it — takes a person only so far before the desire to encounter the Truth overshadows any fear.  Theo appears to be on the right path:  seeking, stumbling, pondering his value and place in the world, rinse and repeat.  The Holy Spirit is with him on this.

I’ve discovered recently, after so many years of being a stubborn Irish, that prayer actually works.  I’ve also recognized that I wasn’t the only one to view prayer as too simple a thing to do yet hardly ever did it.  Finite human logic at its finest.

Praying for Theo is a powerful act and is worth it.

author: @chattigirl6153
text:
Paranormal story similar to your ring! 
The year was 1977 in the town of Pickering On. It was the Christmas season and my mom and dad had an annual Christmas party to attend and mom was getting all dolled up. Mom had her new racy red dress laid out on the end of her bed while she primped and fluffed herself in the bathroom. 
My mom being a very modest lady was playing on the side of sassy with her dress choice and thus very excited to have it.  Once she was all dolled up in her glam she wiggled into her pantyhose and made her way back to her bedroom to put on the coveted racy red dress. Well to her shock, upon her return to the bedroom; No Dress! 
It was gone, poof disappeared. Of course mom assumed my sister or I must have moved her dress. Of course we knew better than to mess with mom’s stuff, so we hadn’t touched her dress. Dad wasn’t home from work at that point so he couldn’t have touched it. This left a tiny miniature poodle as the only other “person” in the house,  he couldn’t reach the bed, nor did he have thumbs to grab it and run so likely not him. We searched, mom spun out, dad played therapist and their night was ruined. Mom ended up in her usual modest navy blue dress and they went off to their party. 
Flash forward 6 years, we move from Pickering to Napanee then to Bowmanville, (dad was relocated for work). We had been in Bowmanville for roughly 7 months, I t’s now Christmas season of 1983. Mom and dad are once again attending the annual Christmas party. Dad is on his way home from work, mom is getting glammed up in the bathroom with her new modest green dress laid out on the bed. Upon her return to her bedroom to get dressed, that racy red dress was laid out on top of her green dress. Just poof! It returned!
Now the back story to the paranormal is, from the time I was born there had been odd happenings in the home. Things that were unexplainable, things would get moved around, knocked over. Unplugged vacuums in the closet would turn on, taps would start to tun when guest were using the powder room. We all saw different shadow people and apparitions. We heard noises, voices and odd misplaced animal sounds. 
One night I woke terrified, locked in my bedroom closet with a bifold door that pushed out from inside to open. I couldn’t get out, it was as if someone was outside my closet holding it shut. My parents were awoken by my blood curdling screams of terror. When they through my closet door open they were expecting me to be injured in some way, what they got was a terrified little girl. I didn’t sleep along in my room after that night, I didn’t tend to go anywhere in the house alone after that night. 
That night was the final straw for my parents, they reached out to the priest of our church at that time for help. The Priest, Bishop and Deacon all tended that house and did some form of Catholic ritual blessing of the house. They buried religious medallions at each corner of the house and also of the property. The house absolutely felt different after that and we never had another incident over the ext 6 years while we lived there. 
To this day, that house in Bowmanville scares me. I don’t know if I would have the nerve as an adult woman to go back to that house. I often wonder if the people who have lived there since ave had any experiences there. 
Needless to say, that racy red dress clearly didn’t impress whatever was inhabiting our home with us, and I’m guessing it liked the green dress even less. Just thought I would share my story, I know most won’t believe it but I can say with everything I experienced during those years that was by far the least scary and unbelievable thing that happened. Cheers lovelies!

author: @Oh-kayKathleen
text:
A glitch: this experience happened a few years ago and it's one of the weirdest things that's ever happened to me. Just thinking about it still makes my stomach drop. So, I was at work and on my laptop, and I pulled my bluetooth mouse out of my tote bag. I use this mouse like every day. So I turn it on, it's connected, and I go to use it and for some reason the right click is stuck. This is one of those Logitech mouses with the covers that connect magnetically so you can get the batteries in and out easily, so after messing with the clicker a little I pull the cover off and lo and behold, I find a dime WEDGED UNDER THE COVER. 

I was finding a lot of dimes at this point in my life, but NEVER like that before. This dime was so far into the mouse that I couldn't see it at all with the cover on, and it's the smallest amount of space possible between the cover and the little gap between the clicker and the body of the mouse, so I genuinely have no idea how the dime would've gotten in there. It's seriously such a tiny gap, there's just no way it could've slipped itself under the cover. It'd take some serious force to get where it was.

I like to think that was one of my grandpas saying hi. I remember having a really hard time when I found that dime and it was just SO strange that it felt like someone was screaming at me that I wasn't alone and that everything would be okay ❤️

I still have that dime on a little trinket tray in my bedroom with some of the other ones I've randomly found, but man that one is special. I'll always cherish my weird little teleporting dime.

author: @TheHylianDragonGirl
text:
I have a lil cat plushie, a beanie baby (who’s sitting next to me on my bed as I speak), and I lost her at the movie theatre parking lot across town from where my home was, back when I was a kid. My mum and I had gone to see a movie (can’t remember the movie, sorry!) earlier in the day, but when we got home, I noticed my cat wasn’t with me. I was super distraught, and though my mum ordered a new one that would come in the mail, I couldn’t let it go. That night, around midnight, I couldn’t sleep and I BEGGED my mum to take me back to the theater and look, and even though I felt bad about it, I NEEDED to check for myself. I can’t remember if my dad went there to look around and found nothing or not, he may have, but I knew I needed to look for her myself.




Low and behold, when we drove up into a parking spot, I don’t even think it was the same place we left either, I’m pretty sure it was across the parking lot, and it was packed with cars.

But ANYWAY, we got out of the car, and I kid you not, like two cars away, she was sitting right there, on the hood of some kind soul’s car. I snatched her up and held her tight, crying happy tears, (and telling my mum repeatedly that I told her so,) and off to home we went, saying prayers of thanks because truly, it shouldn’t have been possible that we found her, but the Lord blessed me in that way. I often use her as a reminder, that miracles can and do happen.☺️


If you read this far, thanks! I hope this brightens someone’s day, even if only for a moment. I love y’all, as does the Lord! We’re here for you, and always know someone somewhere is cheering you on!! Have a wonderful day/night.🥰💜

I asked my mom and sister if it was theirs and they both said no. I had it for years after and then one day I took it off to wash some dishes and it was gone. I don't have a garbage disposal so it couldn't have falling down anything. It just vanished. I guess it was time for it to go back to where it came from.

author: @Thesunmaid
text: I don't think this is a glitch but more of someone saying goodbye. I lost my best friend Jon. He was sick alot...he had sarcoidosis and lymphadema and infections because of both. He passed away and I was pretty sad...I was starting to come out of my depression but I still had this lingering sense of loss and I went to sleep one night and I had a dream that I woke up and it was light out..like..probably about 9 to 10 am and he was sitting on the side of my bed and I was just really happy to see him. I was like"Hi! How are you doing?!" Now when he was sick...his legs were really painful because of the infections and he had to get them wrapped...they would weep and it was just awful for him. And he looked at me and said"I am good...it doesn't hurt anymore." I was just really happy for him and said"That's awesome." he said he had to go and I was like"Ok." I gave him a hug and he walked out. And he was walking really easily...like no pain...he was not out of breath or pale. He looked really healthy. And I woke up a little while later and I just felt like he had come to let me know he was ok and I felt alot better. I am crying as I type this because I really miss him but yeah...Maybe it was just my brain trying to make me feel better but I like to believe it was him letting me know.

text:
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚
بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم
قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّاثَاتِ فِي الْعُقَدِ وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ .
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚
لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مَن ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ عِلْمِهِ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاءَ وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَلَا يَئُودُهُ حِفْظُهُمَا وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْعَظِيمُ (٢٥٥)
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚
🇲🇦🇲🇦الملك 🇲🇦الوطن 🇲🇦الله 🇲🇦🇲🇦
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚
الصحراء في مغربها و المغرب في صحرائه 
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚
 عاشت المملكة المغربية عاش الملك محمد السادس نصره الله و شفاه و عفاه و عاش الجيش المغربي حفظهم الله بما حفظ به الذكر الحكيم لا عاش من خانهم و عزاء للحاقدين والمنافقين الأشرار و لا غالب إلا الله
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚

 اللهم احفظ بلادنا المملكة المغربية و على رأسهاجلالة الملك محمد السادس نصره الله و شفاه و عفاه و أطال في عمره و شعبها و جيشها وكل من يسهر على أمنها و استقرارها حفظهم الله الرحمن الرحيم جميعا بما حفظ به الذكر الحكيم يارب العالمين
تحية احترام و تقدير مغربية لك اخي الكريم فؤاد على هذا الخبر الجميل و الرائع أتمنى لك التوفيق و النجاح الدائم اللهم بلادنا من الفتن ما ظهر منها و ما بطن و اجعلها آمنا مطمئنا يارب العالمين و كل من يريد بالمغرب شرا و سوءا اللهم اجعل كيدهم في نحورهم و شرهم تحت اقدامهم و خد بثأر المغرب منهم يارب العالمين 
 المغرب حاول أن ينتشل الضفدع (افريقيا) من المستنقع لكن الضفدع (أفريقيا) مكانه الطبيعي هو المستنقع 
و تحية لجميع إخواننا في العالم العربي و الإسلامي و العالم بأسره و كل من يتمنى للمملكة المغربية و ملكها شفاه الله و عفاه و أطال لنا في عمره و شعبها الخير و الإزدهار و الإستقرار و ان يبعد عنهم الفتن ما ظهر منها و ما بطن يارب العالمين 
🇲🇦🇲🇦🇲🇦🎉🎉💚💚💚

Ma ville actuelle ❤ je suis venue d’Oujda pour étudier à Rabat à l’âge de 18 ans, et depuis, je ne peux plus la quitter, cette ville est à l’image de mon âme, paisible, pacifique, calme, propre ❤ sans parler de son histoire et sa diversité 😊 
J’ai 40 ans maintenant, et j’y suis toujours, et quand je la quitte pour des missions ou des vacances ou des visites familiales, je ne respire que quand j’y rentre 😊 elle a un secret, un charme dans chaque détail, une énergie qu’on ne trouve nulle part !

text: صحيح تركت لكم ماما فرنسا مدنا أوروبية بمعنى الكلمة، و لكن غباءكم الخام الفريد من نوعه و خياراتكم الجيوسياسية الغريبة ضيعت عليكم أموالا طائلة و ساهمت بشكل كبير في عدم صيانتكم للمدن الفرنسية المتروكة في مقاطعتها الشمال أفريقية مثل الجزائر العاصمة و وهران و قسنطينة فالحيطان بهتت صباغتها البيضاء و المصاعد تعطلت في معظم البنايات و الازبال انتشرت في كل مكان و الحفر في الشوارع. فرنسا ارتكبت جريمة كبرى بأن أهدت لابنتها المراهقة سيارة مرسيدس جديدة بدون تعليمها القيادة، فالنتيجة كانت أن أدخلتها في حائط اسمنتي مسلح  من أول وهلة خلف المقود😂 أما ما تراه في المغرب فهو من صنع مغربي خالص رغم صعوبات البداية، و مازال أمامنا الكثير لننجزه بسواعدنا  الغير مكسورة بكل تواضع، و لا نحتاج للغير لكي نبني بلدنا سواء في الماضي أو الحاضر أو المستقبل 😉

text: تتحدث عن الاندلسيين كانهم نزلوا من السماء الى الجزيرة الايبرية الاندلسيين هم مور يعني مغاربة هجروا الى الجزيرة الايبيرية في فترات مختلفة من ايام طارق ابن زياد وايام عبدالرحمان الداخل الدي استقطب الكثير منهم ولما سقطت الاندلس عادوا الى مكان اجدادهم لان العلاقة كانت دائما بين العدوتين هناك من عاد لتطوان و سلا او مراكش او اسفي  وهناك من عاد لفاس  حسب مكان اجداده الخلاصة الاندلسيون هم مغاربة اصلا والحضارة الاندلسية هي حضارة مغربية في مجملها مع اختلاطهم بالقوط السكان المحليين اما العرب فكانوا قلة قليلة خصوصا بعد عبد الرحمان الداخل الدي قضى على القيسية واليمنية واستقطب امازيغ المغرب بكثرة باغراءات كبيرة هدا هو التاريخ

و هذا يذكرني بآيات من الذكر الحكيم 
 و ما  يقع حاليا  الخلط بين انزال الجديد و الترميم  لتجديد  و الخطاب الملكي اوضح،  قبل هذه الأحداث

لكن يبقى المنفرج   رغم المنعرجات و المسار التنموي  ليس بساط تذروه الرياح بل سجادة راسية متوازنة  بثوابت المملكة  و كذا التقة بين العرش و الشعب  و يبقى تداول الظروف الزمكانية محطات في تاريخ المسار 
اللهم اجعل هذا بلدا امنا مطمئنا أرشدنا الى ما فيه الخير 
و هداية كما  في سورة الفاتحة

text: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم اعوذ بالله من الشيطان الرجيم بسم الله الذي لا يضر مع اسمه شيء في الارض ولا في السماء وهو السميع العليم قل للذين كفرو ستغلبون ستغلبون وتحشرون الى جهنم وبئس المصير  وادوك المنافقين لي خدامين مع إسرائيل جميعا واش محشمتوش المجرمين بعتو راسكم او بعتو دين ديالكوم اليهود وادوك العيالات لي زعما موظفات عند الصهاينة او كيحسبو راسهم مسلمات فراسكم صافي راكم تمسختو او تبهدلتو  واش المنافقين ماشي حشومة عليكم تحسبو راسكم مسلمين او كديرو الخدمة ديال اليهود هادوك المجرمين لي كاينين فالمحاكم جميعا اش كيديرو كيعتاقلو فالمغاربة هادوك المجرمين لي سماوهوم جندارم او البوليس اش كيديرو را كيديرو غير الخدمة ديال اليهود اشمن وراق كيطلبو هاد الإجرام كامل لي كديرو فالمغاربة واش كاين فشي آية قرآنية حاضيين غير ديرو ليهم روشيرش اوعتاقلوهوم او سرقوهوم را الخدمة ديال الإستعمار من بكري را هيا هادي سير نعلة الله وعليكم الى يوم دين وعلى الكافرين والمنافقين جميعا المجرمين ولا تجسس رديتوها  مخابارات  را المسلمين فرض عليهم الله غير الاركان ديال الإسلام او راه لا يمكن واحد بايع دينو او معندو لا جد لا تاسيلا او فنفس الوقت كيدير الخدمة ديال اليهود او قال ليك كيعرف الحق والقانون هاد الخدمة لي كتوقع فالطرقان او بعد الإدارات را مكيديروها ايلا المجرمين او متيحاربش المسلمين ايلا اليهود صافي المجرمين اسقاط نظام صهيوني واسقاط الملكية مابقات لا الإنتخابات لا والو صافي جاء الحق وزهق الباطل ان الباطل كان زهوقا  اقسم بالله بإذن الله وقوة الله ايلا انا هو لي غنكون سباب ديالكوم فالخراب هاهوا كلشي بان ليكم من المجرم ديال محمد السادس باش نوريه استهزء بالله ورسوله لانه صافي را وصلتو البلافو فتاحيماريت المغاربة معذبين فالسوجون او كتحسبوهوم بالإرهاب راه الله لي خلق هاد دنيا ماشي دوك الصهاينة لي كيعطيوكوم تعليمات والله لا بقيتو فيها بإنسكم وجنكم راه الله لي غادي ايدير كلشي انا غنكون غير السبب راه انا قال لينا الله لا تزكو انفسكم را كنهضر مع الحق سبحانه كنتكل  على الحي الذي لا يموت ماشي كنتسنا المجرم ديال فرنسا اعطيني تعليمات ديال الفحشاء والمنكر لي كيسجن المغاربة او كيريب ليهم ديورهوم او كيحسبهوم بالحيوان  احسبوه الصهاينة مسؤول كبير  حورما سيدي ربي نزل جنودك احكم بيننا وبين هاد القوم الظالمين بالحق ولا حول ولا قوة الا بالله العلي العضيم

author: @djfroyd848
text:
أعتقد انو مافيش اغبى من نظام المخزن باع البلاد ولاجل كمشه مستفيدين راح يقترض بلا حدود وانت المروكي قابل لانك موش حر وجاء وقت سداد الفاتورة واش نعمل راك حصلت بوحدك يجيك صندوق النقد يقلك لاتفكر بل انا من يفكر مكانك ارفع اي دعم حتى تسدد الديون عل رايت اغبى من هكذا نظام .اما نظامنا ليس مثالي والذي تصفه بالغبى منع الاقتراض بقانون حتى نكون مستقلين قدفي قرارنا وكله تمويل ذاتي واذا لازم الامر نقترض داخليا احنا في احنا بالدينار الجزائري.انا حقي في الثروه نحس بيه سكن باطل و ملكي مانيش كاري وبسعر رمزي ،الكهرباء والغاز والبنزين و الاتصالات الماء الطرقات شركات وطنيه 100% ماهيش فرنسية او اماراتيه ...،ربما شفت مؤخرا زادو ريال ونصف في البنزين و3ريالات في المازوت الدنيا تقلبت في الجزائر واظراب عام لاننا احرار رفضا هاته الزياده التى لاتذكر 1,5ريال او3ريال لا شيء لكن رفضناها والعظام الذي تسميه غبي تراجع والغى الزياده لانو يستمع لنبض المواطن اما انت مذلول ومطحون ويفعل بك المخزن مايشاء والا القرعة وانت شفت جيل z واش صرالو .انا عارف بلى انت متحسر وقلبك فيه حسد كبير راك مريض ،احنا لو اننا عندنا مخزن نتاعكم ناكلوه اكل من الملك الشفار الاول الى اخنوش . ...الخ يستحيل يبقو يوم .اسمع مليح راح تنحي السروال باغي والا موش باغي مع تعويم الدرهم أجباري وانت اللى عملت في روحك هذي الشيء مافيش واحد جبرك تتسلف عايش بالدين كلشي ×2 والطبقه الوحيده المستفاده هي اصحاب رؤؤس الاموال فقط الشعب اللى باقي سيطحن بقساوة لا تتصور وابقى قول عاش الملك وانا سااساندك وساقول معك عاش الملك وربي يخليه ويطول في عمره باش يطحنك هو واخنوش وجماعتو المستفيدين.ماتجيش تبكي وتقول يالجزائر حلى الحدود انسى .نحن نحب الملك محمد السادس لأنه يقوم بالدور كما ينبغي بدون نحن ان نتدخل ،جشعه مع اخنوش والعصابه جشعهم خلاهم ينفذو بااخلاص المطلوب منو فيكم ،فما تقدرش تقول انو الجزائر هى اللى تطحن فيكم هذا غير صحيح هذا ما يصنع الجاهل والغبي بنفسه.امل مروكي وجد فرصه للهرب سيهرب بجلده لانو يستحيل العيش في المغرب كلشي×2 .
السؤال من هو النظام الغبي برايك ؟!!احنا ماشيين الى400 مليار دولار ناتج خام مع نهايه 2026 انشاء الله وانت ابقى في 170مليار دولار مع 107,9مليار دولار ديون هل ترى الفرق. صندوق النقد الدولى يقول يتوقع وصل المغرب 390مليار دولار ناتج خام سمع 2040 بعد 14سنه شفت الفارق هو عندك ،وكم سيكون عدد السكان المغرب اكثر من خمسين مليون حينها ونحن يتوقع نصل مابين 800الى900مليار دولار وعدد السكان يقرا 70مليون لانو انو فيه دعم للاسره والزواج والانجاب.وبماذا الصندوق  وصل الى800_900مليار دولار لانو درس المشاريع الكبرى كالحديد والفوسفاط غلزنك والنحاس والاتربه النادره و الطاقه الرخيسه وقاع الضرايب هي مشاريع ستقلب الموازين وتغير نهاءيا وجه قوه الاقتصاد الجزائري مع استمرار هاته السياسة دون اقتراض والصناعا العسكرية وتوطينها في الجزائر والجيش راح يمول نفسو بنفسه وراح ياخذ جزء بسيط من الميزانية وزين الفلاحه والاكتفاء الذاتي والصندوق متواضع في الرقم الذي وصل إليه هاته ليست مشاريع بسبطه تجيب مليار او اثنين داخله ومرتبطه بقطاعات اخرى حساسه،زيظ الاسمنت الذي يجعل الجزائر اول مصدر في افريقيا ،انشاء الله قبل 2030 سنصبح اول اقتصاد افريقي .كل هذا التخطيط يوثقه صندوق النقد الدولى ويؤكده وهذا ماخططه النظام الغبي بنظرك.سؤالي لك انت وينك من كل مايجري ؟!!؟راح تبقى تجري وراء البوطه ولتر البنزين وفقدان الردهم لقيمته.احنا الدينار نتاعنا عندو مده وهو يعوم والشعبي والدوله تاقلمت معه ومافيش صدمات الان ،العمل وفقط.

في الإقتصاد، التعويم لا يعني بالضروري ارتفاع التضخم و ارتفاع الأسعار و
